phrases.intent_en: Ask for a recommendation for a good terrace café
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous me recommander un bon café avec une belle terrasse ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY voo muh ruh-ko-mon-DAY un bon kah-FAY ah-VEK OON bel teh-RASS
phrases.cultural_note: Rue Saint-Denis is lined with terrasse cafés especially in summer. Locals linger for hours — don't rush your coffee.

responses.english: Try the café on the corner, nice terrace.
responses.phonetic: eh-say-YAY luh kah-FAY oh KWAHN BEL tay-RASS
responses.french: Essayez le café au coin, belle terrasse.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where a good used bookstore is
phrases.text_local: Il y a une librairie de livres usagés dans le coin ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel ee ah OON lee-breh-REE duh LEE-vruh oo-zah-ZHAY dahn luh KWAN
phrases.cultural_note: Saint-Denis is known for its independent bookshops and literary culture. Several French-language used bookshops line the street.

responses.english: There is one on Saint-Laurent Boulevard.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ahn ah oon soor luh bool-VAR sahn-loh-RAHN
responses.french: Il y en a une sur le boulevard Saint-Laurent.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for directions to the Latin Quarter
phrases.text_local: Le Quartier latin, c'est dans quelle direction ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh kar-tee-YAY lah-TAN say dahn kel dee-rek-SYON
phrases.cultural_note: The Latin Quarter around UQAM university along Saint-Denis is Montréal's intellectual hub, with theatres, bars, and cultural venues.

responses.phonetic: say too DRWAH vair luh SOOD
responses.french: C'est tout droit, vers le sud.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a restaurant has a table available
phrases.text_local: Avez-vous une table de libre pour deux personnes ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-VAY voo OON TAHBL duh LEE-bruh poor duh pair-SON
phrases.cultural_note: Saint-Denis has dozens of restaurants of every cuisine. Reservations are recommended for Friday and Saturday evenings.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find a dépanneur for snacks
phrases.text_local: C'est où le dépanneur le plus proche ?
phrases.pronunciation: say OO luh day-pan-UR luh ploo PROSH
phrases.cultural_note: 'Dépanneur' (dep) is the Québécois term for corner store / convenience store. They're on almost every block and sell beer, snacks, and essentials.

responses.english: On the corner, to the right.
responses.phonetic: oh KWAHN duh lah ROO ah DRWAHT
responses.french: Au coin de la rue, à droite.
